rule: monitor (CDS3.5) deletion removes ~4084 lines, ~198KB; ~5x larger XML than controllers (variables 294, IoT meta 147, logging 128); auto-cleanup of NetworkMappings CAN refs
network deletion removes Network element at all 3 locations (Networks/NetworkMappings/Editor.Networks) with same Guid; member CAN ports + their BitRate values are preserved on owning devices

network = CANopen cluster: each 2-member network auto-creates 2 Monitoring nodes with 8 CANopen params each (ConsumerTime, KeepAlive, IsMandatory, NoNmtStart, AutoMonitor, ResetNode, IsActive, Monitors); .exp gets G_CAN<n>_MonitoredNodes + HbConsumerNodes + CSDOinstances + CANopenConsumeHB() + SDOManager.Init()

2-member network detachment causes -719 bytes delta on each member's .exp (cleanup of MonitoredNodes + HbConsumerNodes + CSDOinstances + 2 function calls); deterministic across NETWORK2 and NETWORK1 deletions
device deletion in MultiTool does NOT delete its .exp file from disk; .exp persists as orphan until user manually removes or Export is re-run on remaining devices
each network gets a unique active color (#FF33CCFF NETWORK1, #FF007CC2 NETWORK2, #FF003882 NETWORK3); inactive nodes become #FFEDEEEE
Meta element has separate Modified (last save) and Exported (last .exp generation) timestamps; SystemVersion increments per event (Save +1, Export +1)

<CommunicatesWithGuid> is always 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 (null UUID) regardless of network membership; this field is NOT how network membership is encoded — use NetworkMappings/Network/CAN[@Guid] instead
<Bitrate> (lowercase r) on Network element + <BitRate> (uppercase R) on each member CAN must stay synchronized; both updated together on network BitRate change
